title: podman-remote build, with preprocessor: warnings go to server, not client
body:
Another one of those cases where output is seen by podman server, but not the client.

In window 1:
```console
$ podman system service --timeout=0
```
In window 2:
```console
$ mkdir aaa
$ printf "FROM scratch\n\n#error\n" >aaa/Warn.in
$ printf "FROM scratch\n\n#include \"nonesuch\"\n" >aaa/Err.in
$ ./bin/podman-remote build -q -t foo -f Warn.in aaa
d6f2a9b367c5d6a3851225517e055430cf63042b310bbaa8373d96e92a70d4c4
$ ./bin/podman-remote build -q -t foo -f Err.in aaa
1339dc1a137603d0dcfe34c561c69323c4a41545220d15543e0716d0501f5963
```
Back to window 1:
```
WARN[0004] Ignoring <stdin>:3:2: error: #error

WARN[0006] Ignoring <stdin>:4:2: fatal error: nonesuch: No such file or directory
compilation terminated.
```
In all cases, exit status is 0. I'm not really OK with that, but that's what buildah does, so (shrug).

comments.body: I think we need Buildah changes for this one - it looks like these errors are not making it through the writer that Podman passes in and instead are being logged directly. @TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind PTAL.

> In all cases, exit status is 0. I'm not really OK with that, but that's what buildah does, so (shrug).

This was in part of #3229.
